Q1 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding India-Russia Relations:
1. Russia was India’s largest crude oil supplier in FY 2024–25.
2. Exercise INDRA is a bilateral military exercise between India and Russia.
3. India’s bilateral trade with Russia is export-dominated, led by pharmaceuticals and textiles.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
India’s bilateral trade with Russia is import-dominated, led by crude oil.

Q2 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

With reference to the Industrial Park Rating System (IPRS) 3.0, consider the following statements:
1. It has been developed by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) with support from the Asian Development Bank (ADB).
2. Parameters such as sustainability, green infrastructure, logistics connectivity, digitalization, and skill linkages have been newly included in the framework.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• The Industrial Park Rating System (IPRS) 3.0 is a new upgraded framework introduced by
the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) with support from the
Asian Development Bank (ADB) to benchmark and improve the quality of industrial parks
and estates across India.
• IPRS 3.0 expands on previous versions by including new, forward-looking parameters that
reflect the evolving needs of industries and sustainability goals:
♦ Sustainability and Green Infrastructure: This includes criteria related to environmental
management, energy efficiency, water conservation, waste management, and pollution
control, ensuring industrial parks adopt eco-friendly practices.
♦ Logistics Connectivity: Measures the connectivity of the park in terms of road, rail, air,
and port access, providing industries situated there with efficient supply chain and
distribution options.
♦ Digitalization: Emphasizes adoption of digital technologies for park management,
services, and operations, enhancing efficiency and transparency.
♦ Skill Linkages: Focuses on the availability and development of skilled labor within and
around the park, ensuring industries have access to the talent pool they need.

Q4 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

‘Tondon Committee’ is related to which of the following?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Recently, the Crime in India 2023 report was published by the National Crime Records Bureau
(NCRB).
About NCRB
• Establishment: 1986 on recommendations of the Tandon Committee, National Police
Commission (1977-81), and MHA Task Force (1985).
• Parent Ministry: Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA).
• Functions:
♦ Acts as the national repository of crime and criminal data.
♦ Manages the Crime and Criminal Tracking Network & Systems (CCTNS).
♦ Publishes three major reports: Crime in India, Accidental Deaths & Suicides, and Prison
Statistics.

The term SAMBHAV, recently seen in the news, refers to:

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

(Secure Army Mobile Bharat Version).

Q6 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

With reference to the ‘Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025’, consider the following statements:
1. It bans all real money games (RMGs), regardless of whether they are skill-based or chancebased.
2. It makes a clear legal distinction between games of skill and games of chance.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Recently, the Parliament of India has passed the ‘Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming
Act, 2025’, to regulate and prohibit certain gaming formats having social and economic concerns.
• The Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025 bans all real money games (RMGs),
regardless of whether they are skill-based or chance-based. It prohibits advertising of real
money games, especially those involving monetary stakes.
• The Act does not distinguish between skill and chance; eroding the legal precedent and
potentially violating Article 19(1)(g), which protects the right to trade and occupation.

Q7 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

With reference to the Registrar General of India (RGI), consider the following statements:
1. The RGI was established in 1961 and functions under the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation.
2. It is responsible for conducting the decennial Census of India, which has been held regularly since 1951.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• The Registrar General of India (RGI) recently asked States to take steps towards achieving universal registration of births and deaths.
• The Registrar General of India (RGI) was established in 1961 by the Government of India.
• It works under the administrative control of the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA).
• It conducts the decennial Census of India (since 1872; regular since 1881) and is responsible
for planning, coordinating, and supervising census operations.

Q10 Not checked

Consider the following statements regarding Utilitarian approach to ethics:
1. It is concerned with maximising the benefit and minimising the harm that arises from our decisions.
2. It motivates individual to act in a particular way.
3. It is concerned with the consequences of our decisions.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Utilitarianism is an ethical theory that determines right from wrong by focusing on outcomes. It
is a form of consequentialism. Utilitarianism holds that the most ethical choice is the one that
will produce the greatest good for the greatest number.

Q15 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding Ecotone:
1. Ecotone is a zone of junction between two or more diverse ecosystems.
2. Ecotone is always smaller than a biome.
3. A well-developed ecotone may contain some unique organisms which might be absent in the adjacent ecosystems.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Ecotone is a zone of junction between two or more diverse ecosystems. For example, the
mangrove forests represent an ecotone between marine and terrestrial ecosystem.
• It may be very narrow or quite wide, but smaller than a biome which is a much larger entity.
• Well-developed ecotones contain some organisms which are entirely different from that of
the adjoining communities.

Solution

• As compared to the assessment of ISFR 2021, there has been an increase in the total forest and tree cover of the country, as reported in the ISFR 2023. The total forest and tree cover increased from 24.62% in 2021 to 25.17% in 2023.

2. Biomethanation
3. Incineration

Q26 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements :
1. Dynamic Random-Access Memory (DRAM) is slower than Static Random-Access Memory (SRAM).
2. Cache and Memory Registers are part of Central Processing Unit (CPU), while Regular Random- Access Memory (RAM) is not its part.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• In SRAM, flip-flop circuit is used to store the data which can be accessed almost instantly
whereas DRAM uses a capacitor to store data which requires constant refreshing to maintain
the charge on the capacitor. Thus, Dynamic Random-Access Memory (DRAM) is slower than
Static Random-Access Memory (SRAM).
• Cache and Memory Registers are part of Central Processing Unit (CPU), while Regular Random-
Access Memory (RAM) and Hard Disk are not its part. It acts as temporary storage area that
is more readily available to the processor than the computer’s main memory source i.e. RAM
or ROM.

Q30 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following types of software:
1. Search engines
2. Operating system
3. Game engines
Which of the above software can be classified as system software?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
System Software is the software that is directly related to coordinating computer operations and
performs tasks associated with controlling and utilizing computer hardware. These programs
assist in running application programs and are designed to control the operation of a computer
system. A search engine is an application software and not system software that is designed to
carry out web searches. Hence, option (b) is correct.

Q31 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Cloud computing is the on-demand availability of computer system resources.
2. Virtualization is a cloud computing technique, which allows the sharing of a single physical instance of an application or resource among multiple organizations or tenants (customers).
3. Utility computing is a cloud computing technique based on the Pay-per-Use model.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Cloud Computing provides means of accessing the applications as utilities over the Internet.
Utility computing is a subset of cloud computing, where computing resources are provided on
demand based on the Pay-per-Use model. Virtualization in cloud computing allows multiple
operating systems and applications to run simultaneously on the same hardware, which allows
the sharing of a single physical instance of an application or resource among multiple organizations
or tenant. Hence, all the given statements are correct.

Q37 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which type of modularity exists when a family of dissimilar products uses the same assembly or component?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Component sharing modularity: Family of dissimilar products uses the same assembly or components.
Cut to fit modularity: A component parameters or features can be adjusted to provide a variety of products.
Component swapping modularity: Modularity in a product that is differentiated only by a single component/assembly.
Platform modularity: Products that consists of different combination of modules assembled on a same basic structure.

Which of the following is an example of passive fire protection system?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Passive fire protection system is an integral component of structural fire protection and fire
safety in a building. They does not require any action or motion in order to work in the event of fire.
Example: Fire resistance wall, fire resistant glass, closures, fire proof cladding etc.

Which of the above statements is/are true?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Quality circles originated in Japan. It is a group of workers who voluntarily meet to discuss ways
of improving products or process. Quality circles are usually less structured and more informal
than task forces entrusted with a project. The working staffs involved in quality circles meet
frequently either at someone’s home or at the plant before shift begins.

Q47 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following product quality management processes ensures that the end product conforms to functional requirements at a particular stage of construction?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Quality planning aims at identifying the quality requirements and standards that are relevant
to the product and documenting methods for determining, how the project will conform to
these requirements and standards.
• Quality assurance focuses on demonstrating the planned and systematic confidence building
measures, to ensure that the necessary quality requirements and standards are being met in
practice.
• Quality control is the operational technique of monitoring and recording the results of
execution to assess performance and make necessary changes in processes (where required)
to ensure conformance to the requirements and standards.
• Quality inspection determines the acceptability of the end product or deliverable or service
or a stage of construction, to ensure that it conforms to functional requirements.
• Quality audit is the in-house or third party’s systematic and independent examination and
documentation to determine whether product quality activities and related results comply
with the planned arrangements and are implemented effectively to achieve project objectives.

Q48 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Non-availability of antivirus software, server breakdown and incompatibility of software with purchased hardware are examples of:

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Cost overrun risks: Inaccurate cost estimates, inadequate cost planning and control, no extra
work control, no analysis of constantly changing market conditions, and incomplete project closure.
Technology risks: Inadequate information on new technology, non-replacement of old technology,
non-availability of competent and professional staff to use new technology, and lack of managerial
skills.
Organisational risks: Inappropriate organisation network, poor assignment/allocation of tasks
and responsibilities, lack of competent persons, no project manual/documented procedures, project
being too complex for the resources available, inadequate communications infrastructure, wrong
selection of project management, no database, inflexible meetings, inability to take timely corrective
action, and incomplete project closure.
Communication and network failure risks: Non-availability of antivirus software to safeguard
office computers, loss of data due to network breakdown, no back up of data available, vendors
not competent enough, landline phones not working properly, lack of resources, high electricity
fluctuations, internet servers breakdown, installation of wrong computer equipment, noncompatibility
of software with the purchased hardware, and ineffective information management
system.

Q50 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In heuristic rules for resource allocation in context to resource smoothing, when more jobs compete for the same resource, priority is given to:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The essential rules are:
1. The resources are to be allocated serially in time i.e., resource allocation should start on the
first day; all possible jobs are to be scheduled for the first day before moving to the second
day and so on.
2. When more jobs compete for the same resource, preference is to be given to the job/jobs with
least float.
3. Jobs once started should continue till they are finished i.e., breaking of jobs is not allowed.
4. Whenever possible, non-critical jobs are to be postponed so that critical jobs can be scheduled
without increasing the project completion time.

Q51 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The Letter of Intent in contracting:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
This is a letter issued to the successful tenderer intimating about his/her offer being accepted.
LOI is issued before signing of the contract by the two parties. The successful tenderer is required
to intimate any pre-condition within the stipulated time. Issue of LOI by the project promoters
and acceptance of the same by the successful tenderer does not obviate the need for signing a
contract. Since LOI is not a legal document, the two parties have to necessarily execute the contract
by signing the contract agreement.

Solution

(c)
The APMBok (Association of project managers body of knowledge) defines the project sponsor as,
(i) Individual or body for whom the project is undertaken, the primary risk taker.
(ii) The individual representing the sponsoring body and to whom the project manager reports.
(iii) A person or organization providing funds for the project.
Note : The project manager is appointed by the project sponsor and the project sponsor is appointed
by portfolio manager or CEO.

Q59 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The relative dielectric constant of a solid dielectric in alternating field is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
At low frequencies, ions have enough time to respond to the changing electric field. Hence, at
low frequencies, all the four types of polarisations are present and the value of the total polarisation
is very high, leading to maximum value of εr , and stop contributing to the total polarization. At
high frequencies, the Orientational and Ionic polorizations cannot keep up with the rapid field
reversa and mainly Electronic Polarization is present. Thus, the total polarization decreases leading
to εr approaching unity at high frequencies. Therefore, the relative dielectric constant of a solid
dielectric in alternating field is maximum at the power frequencies and decreases to unity at
frequencies in the ultraviolet range.

1. Fermi level lies below the donor level at room temperature T.
2. Fermi level lies above the donor level as T → 0.

Solution

Q62 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Electronic polarization increases due to increase in temperature.
2. Orientational polarization in dielectric is inversly proportional to temperature.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Electronic polarization is independent of temperature.
• As temperature increases, the thermal motion of the molecules also increases, making it harder
for the dipoles to align with the electric field. Thus, Orientational polarization in dielectric
decreases with increase in temperature.
Hence, only statement 2 is correct.

Q63 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding magnetic materials:
1. Value of spontaneous magnetisation for a paramagnetic material is zero.
2. Diamagnetic materials have small permanent dipole moment.
3. In ferromagnetic materials, total magnetic flux density is summation of flux density due to applied field and flux density due to magnetization.
Which of the above given statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Diamagnetic materials do not have permanent dipole moment.
• Paramagnetic materials do not exhibit spontaneous magnetization in the absence of an external
magnetic field. Whereas Ferromagnetic materials exhibit spontaneous magnetization. We have,
B = μ0(H + M) i.e. for ferromagnetic materials, the total magnetic flux density is the summation
of the flux density due to the applied field (H) and the flux density due to the material's magnetization (M).
Hence, statements 1 and 3 are correct.

Q64 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

“Manganin” an alloy of copper and manganese , is most prominently used in which of the following applications?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Manganin is an alloy of copper, manganese and nickel known for its low temperature coefficient
of resistance, high electrical resistivity, and low thermal EMF, making it ideal for use in precision
resistors. Thus, it is used as standard resistance manufacturing material. Standard resistances are
used for measurement purposes, etc.

Q65 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which material is used as transducer in working of microphone?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Piezoelectric materials are used in microphone that convert sound energy (mechanical pressure
variations from sound waves) into electrical signals.

Hexagonal closed packed space lattice (HCP) is found in

Correct answer: A

Q67 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements
1. Chain growth polymers are those polymers which are formed by successive addition of monomer units to the growing chain.
2. Chain growth polymers are formed by a process which involves chain reactions and the process is called chain growth polymerisation.
Which of the statements is/are true?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Chain growth polymers are those polymers which are formed by successive addition of monomer
units to the growing chain having a reactive intermediate (free radical, carbocation or carboanions)
at one end of the chain. The chain reaction process is called chain growth polymerization.

Q69 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The aluminium alloy made by melting aluminium with 2 to 10% magnesium and 1.75% copper is called

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The aluminium alloy made by melting aluminium with 2 to 10% magnesium and 1.75% copper is
called magnalium. This alloy is known for its light weight and good mechanical properties, making
it suitable for use in aircraft and automobile components.

Q70 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Endurance limit is the primary concern in the design of
1. Rotating shaft
2. Machine base
3. Column
4. Industrial structure
Which of the above is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Endurance limit is defined as the maximum stress level which when applied to a component for
infinite number of loading and unloading cycles, will not result in a failure due to fatigue. Fatigue
is a failure when a component is subjected to fluctuating stress such as a rotating shaft. Whereas
Machine base, Column, and Industrial structure are primarily subjected to static or steady loads.

Q71 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Enviro-Bond polymer is used for water cleanup. Which of the following are correct about it?
1. It is hydrophobic in nature.
2. Its density is more than density of water.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Enviro-Bond polymer is used for water cleanup. It is hydrophobic (does not like water) and its
density is lower than the density of water so it float at the oil-water interface.
Polypropylene is also used for absorbing the oil from the ocean surface.

Correct answer: A
Solution

youngest daughter of the Roys. So, the surname of Rashmi is Sharma. Hence, the answer is (a).

backup component.

Q92 Not checked

(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I) : Single stroke lettering is just freehand writing.
Statement (II): Single stroke lettering is done without lifting the pencil.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Single stroke means the thickness of letters should be uniform.
The letter is made of one or more stems or curves, each of which is made of a single stroke. It
does not mean that pencil will not be lifted during lettering.

Q93 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I) : Kernel is the core component of the operating system that interacts directly with the hardware.
Statement (II) : Kernel in Operating System is implemented using Machine Level Language (MLL).

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Kernel is the core component of the operating system that interacts directly with the hardware.
The Kernel mode is also called Central Mode or Privileged Mode, and taken as Master Program
staying close to Hardware and regulating its use. In Kernel mode, the executing code has
complete and unrestricted access to the underlying hardware.
• Kernel is typically implemented using a combination of high-level and low-level programming
languages such as C and assembly, not strictly machine language. Hence, Statement (II) is
false.

Q99 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:

Statement (I): Disaster Risk Index (DRI) covers hazards, vulnerabilities, and exposures, with population and GDP being the two main exposure parameters.
Statement (II): This index was developed by the 15th Finance Commission to bring objectivity in fiscal federalism by taking note of disaster risk faced by states for resource allocation.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• The Disaster Risk Index (DRI) was developed by the 15th Finance Commission to bring
objectivity in fiscal federalism by taking note of disaster risk faced by states for resource allocation.
• The DRI covers 14 hazards (such as earthquake, cyclone, flood, drought), 14 vulnerabilities
(like rural/ urban poor, vulnerable children and women), and 2 exposure parameters - population and GDP.

Q100 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): H-1B visa is designed to fill specialised skill gaps in the US labour market where qualified Americans are unavailable.
Statement (II): China is the biggest beneficiary of the H-1B program followed by India.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• An H-1B visa is a non-immigrant visa in the United States that allows US companies to
temporarily employ foreign workers in specialty occupations. These are jobs that typically
require technical expertise, a bachelor’s degree or higher, or its equivalent in fields like IT,
engineering, science, medicine, and finance.
• It is designed to fill specialised skill gaps in the US labor market where qualified Americans are unavailable.
• Indians have been the biggest beneficiaries of the H-1B program, accounting for more than
70% of successful H-1B petitions followed by China, who account for 12-13%.
